content.text: Now let me check the CSS for code block styling.

The issue is that `.code-block` sets a dark background (`#1e1e2e`) but only `pre` elements inside it get the light text colour (`#cdd6f4`). Any other text (e.g. ``, `

`, or bare text) inherits the default dark colour from Pico CSS.

content.content: The file /home/ubuntu/charming-with-claude/experiments/2026-03-13-code-review-skills-our-data/charming-analysis/review-tool/review_tool/static/style.css has been updated successfully.

content.text: Added `color: #cdd6f4` to `.code-block` so all text inside inherits the light colour, and added a `.code-block code` rule to make inline `<code>` elements transparent (overriding Pico's default dark background/colour for code elements). This should make all text in code blocks readable against the dark background.
